title: God Gave To Me A Child In Part by Robert Louis Stevenson
content:
GOD gave to me a child in part,
Yet wholly gave the father's heart:
Child of my soul, O whither now,
Unborn, unmothered, goest thou?

You came, you we

title: Since Thou Hast Given Me This Good Hope, O God by Robert Louis Stevenson
content:
SINCE thou hast given me this good hope, O God,
That while my footsteps tread the flowery sod
And the great woods embower me, and white dawn

title: Men Are Heaven's Piers by Robert Louis Stevenson
content:
MEN are Heaven's piers; they evermore
Unwearying bear the skyey floor;
Man's theatre they bear with ease,
Unfrowning cariatides!

title: Good and Bad Children by Robert Louis Stevenson
content:
Children, you are very little,
And your bones are very brittle;
If you would grow great and stately,
You must try to walk sedately.

title: Flower God, God Of The Spring by Robert Louis Stevenson
content:

Cold-dyed shield in the sky, lover of versicles,
